summary: Follow-up to MPI-156 perf resolution: warm-on-connect (hide ~3:30 cold tax) + remove MpiClearVram node 58 (stage-2 40GB re-fault). Node-58 needs live browser-bypass test + 24GB VRAM-peak check before JSON edit.
Picked up MPI-157; ran the node-58 live test on a 5090 Pod.

OPT 2 (remove MpiClearVram node 58) DISPROVEN LIVE on a 5090 Pod. Bypassed node 58 in LTX_t2v.json (rewired 104+126 to node 52): warm 1s/1K t2v ran 119.42s vs 45s baseline (2.6x slower), first stage-2 step 69 s/it. aimdo re-stages the 40GB at stage-2 regardless of node 58; the node's unload_all_models() is load-bearing for SPEED (clean-slate stage-2 fault). Reverted the JSON edit. Keep node 58. OPT 1 (warm-on-connect) still open.
Re-scoped to warm-on-connect only (node-58 removal DISPROVEN+dropped). Parked to todo - real optimization, not urgent. Re-titled.

summary: POD GGUF BLOCKER diagnosed (screenshot, live 5090 Pod): ComfyUI-GGUF node import FAILS on the Pod with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'gguf'`. TWO gaps: (1) the Pod image's node_lock.json (mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/node_lock.json) is a STALE COPY — it does NOT have the ComfyUI-GGUF entry I added to the app's dev_configs/node_lock.json, so the image build never installed the node OR its gguf pip dep (per the bump-rebuild law: node_lock edit = rebuild BOTH images). (2) The per-model VOLUME-install path (routes/remoteModels.js remoteInstallDep, line 284) only passes install_command to the wrapper when dep.installRequirementsCommand (a CUSTOM command) is set — it SILENTLY DROPS installRequirements:true (the standard requirements.txt flag). ComfyUI-GGUF uses installRequirements:true → so even though the wrapper pushed the node folder to the volume, it never ran `pip install -r requirements.txt` → no gguf pkg → import fails. FIX OPTIONS: (a) add ComfyUI-GGUF to the Pod node_lock.json + rebuild the Pod image (bakes node+gguf in, but ComfyUI-GGUF is MODEL-specific not universal — does it belong baked? the image loop installs ALL node_lock nodes regardless of installOnEngine, so adding it there WOULD bake it = acceptable for now); AND/OR (b) FIX remoteModels.js so installRequirements:true also triggers a remote pip install -r requirements.txt (the wrapper must support running requirements.txt for a volume node, not just a custom command) — this is the GENERAL fix so future model-specific nodes with a requirements.txt work remotely. (b) is the real bug. Detail in handoff 60c42614.

SHIPPED the bf16-local/GGUF-Pod split (commit 443a25f). Pod A/B DECIDED: GGUF Q8 wins on Pod (cold once/boot ~4:29, then 22-25s warm across model jumps; 15180MB Staged vs bf16 40050MB). generate_ltx.py fans 4->8 via Input_Use_GGUF; commandExecutor _toGgufFilename swaps per-gen (hard engine rule, forceLocal-keyed so concurrent local+remote each get right weights); dependencies split into ltx23-transformer-bf16(engine:local)+gguf(engine:remote); filterDepsByEngine at 4 boundaries; Q8 GGUF re-hosted HF->R2 (models.cubric.studio, verified 200+size-match); 10/10 resolver tests pass. NEW FINDING (RAM-floor): GGUF warm-win needs ~90GB system RAM — on a 60GB 5090 the RAM cache thrashes -> warm degrades to cold-class (2:14-8:13 at 3s/2K). Working set ~40-45GB at quality floor (Gemma fp8 14.5GB + Q8 transformer 15GB, neither shrinkable). Filed MPI-160 (wait-for-high-RAM Pod via auto-retry; flat price = free to wait). mpi-ci Pod node_lock ComfyUI-GGUF bake-fix STAGED (durable gguf-missing cure; needs Pod image rebuild). REMAINING: pick Unsloth vs QuantStack + delete bf16 template backup; user CPU-Pod download retest from R2.

Won't-do. Warm-on-connect strategy dropped (not attempting). Node-58 half already disproven. The GGUF-transformer cold-tax lever this card drifted into already SHIPPED under MPI-168 (Q8-GGUF on balanced-LTX Pod, live-proven). Nothing left to build.
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
